title: El Nino and India source map
articleIntent: A long, data-rich article explaining how El Nino changes India's monsoon risk, what history shows, and where the evidence is uncertain.

availableNow.notes: ONI gives seasonal Nino 3.4 SST anomalies. Phase tags in the artifact are mechanical thresholds; official event labels require persistence and coupled atmospheric signals.

availableNow.sourceName: Our World in Data / Copernicus ERA5
availableNow.notes: Useful for broad annual rainfall swings, but not a canonical IMD monsoon rainfall series.

availableNow.evidenceNeed: Canonical all-India and homogeneous-region southwest monsoon rainfall departure
availableNow.sourceName: India Meteorological Department, Climate Prediction and Monitoring Group
availableNow.notes: Public IMD Pune HTML table parsed by scripts/ingest-imd-monsoon-rainfall.mjs. Includes actual rainfall and percent departure for June, July, August, September, and June-September for all-India and four homogeneous regions.

availableNow.sourceName: India Meteorological Department, Climate Prediction and Monitoring Group
availableNow.coverage: 1901 to 2025, with early-year gaps in some subdivisions
availableNow.notes: Public IMD Pune subdivision rainfall pages parsed by scripts/ingest-imd-subdivision-rainfall.mjs. Includes monthly and annual actual rainfall for 36 meteorological subdivisions, plus 1971-2020 normal values in metadata.
Good for local examples and reader-facing city comparisons. Not a replacement for IMD gridded rainfall.

availableNow.sourceName: World Bank WDI / ILO modelled estimates
availableNow.notes: Use for the share of workers in agriculture and why monsoon risk matters.
Use food, vegetables, cereals, pulses, oils, milk, onion, tomato and potato inflation/index series for the economic consequences section.
Useful for event narrative and death/damage case studies, not the core ENSO-rainfall relationship.

availableNow.evidenceNeed: Chart-ready ENSO and Indian monsoon join
availableNow.sourceName: Derived from NOAA ONI and IMD SW Monsoon Rainfall Data
availableNow.notes: Created by scripts/derive-enso-imd-monsoon.mjs. Joins MJJ/JJA/JAS/ASO ONI metrics and mechanical official-event flags to IMD June-September rainfall and departure for each region.

needsIngestion.evidenceNeed: Daily gridded rainfall for maps and extremes
needsIngestion.notes: POSTing RF25=2024 to https://imdpune.gov.in/cmpg/Griddata/RF25.php returned a 25 MB NetCDF file. High-value for maps and district/state rollups, but requires NetCDF parsing, grid masks, and careful storage policy.
Required as a caveat: positive IOD can offset El Nino's drying tendency for India, as in 1997.
Useful for the 'rainfall is not the same as water security' section.

needsIngestion.evidenceNeed: Crop production and yield by year/state
needsIngestion.notes: Needed for rice, pulses, oilseeds, sugarcane, cotton, and coarse cereals.

needsIngestion.preferredSource: GDELT, newspaper archives, Factiva/LexisNexis if available, official IMD press releases
needsIngestion.notes: For 'what people were told at the time' and policy response timelines. Official press releases are useful; media archives may require subscription access.
